changes.before: "You had fever in spring," Sylvanas said. "Half a dozen sick for a week, one tanner losing toes after the rot set in, and then it stopped. This spreads faster, goes for the gut, and keeps going. That's different."
changes.after: "That child is being held upright to stand," Sylvanas said. "Your bread seller is coughing over the loaves, and I can smell the sickness from here. We came from Millhaven. This is different."

changes.before: "A man on the road. He paid me to watch the back half. Said if anyone opened it, I was to make sure they didn't leave with answers."
changes.after: "I don't know," Davan said again, nearer to breaking now. "Please."

changes.before: "Yes," Jaina said, hearing the line again now that it was too late. "That was what he meant."
changes.after: Jaina sat back on her heels. "We should have searched harder."
